status: RUN 2026-06-12. BSBM 100M (pc=284826) headline throughput matrix, Fluree v4.0.6. explore + businessIntelligence x {1,4,8,16,32} clients. Explore: 0 timeouts; BI: 0 timeouts.

dataset.name: BSBM 100M (pc=284826, forward-chained)
dataset.description: Berlin SPARQL Benchmark e-commerce dataset, canonical 100M scale point (headline). Deterministic generator (no seed); pin = toolkit SHA + -pc + flags.
dataset.entities: 284,826 products (5 types, root ProductType1 matches all due to -fc forward chaining); ~5.7M offers; ~2.85M reviews

methodology.notes: Update use case not run at 100M (only the 1M dataset was generated with -ud / dataset_update.nt). BI throughput is low because the driver's random param draw frequently hits root type ProductType1 (matches ALL products due to -fc forward chaining) -> full-scan analytic queries. This is a known generator/methodology artifact, not an engine ceiling; leaf-type BI queries are fast. See REPORT.md.

fluree.label: Fluree
fluree.config: default server config; LeafletCache budget ~21.5 GiB

import.notes: fluree create bsbm100 --from td_100m/dataset.nt -> 100,000,748 flakes; prior session measured ~166 s (0.60M flakes/s, 12.8 GB peak).

caveats: Real BSBM via bsbmtools-0.2 (randomized query mix, QMpH).
Absolute QMpH is comparable engine-vs-engine on this box, not across toolkit versions/scales.
BI@100M is dominated by the -fc root-type (ProductType1) draw; absolute BI QMpH understates engine capability on selective (leaf-type) analytic queries.
